basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Yā Sīn — called “the heart of the Qurʾān” — opens with two enigmatic letters that Qushayrī reads as a name of the Prophet and as an oath of love: the yāʾ points to the Day of the primordial Covenant, the sīn to God's secret with His beloved. God swears by the wise Qurʾān that Muḥammad is indeed among the messengers, upon a straight path. The sūra confronts the great denial of resurrection and unfolds its proofs: the dead earth the rain revives, the night from which the day is stripped, the sun and moon each floating in its orbit — where Qushayrī reads the changing states of the servant (talwīn) and the stability of the established knower (tamkīn). At its center shines the parable of the town: three messengers denied, then the man who came running from the far end of the city, who believes, bears witness, is slain, and enters the Garden still wishing for the salvation of his people. It culminates upon the secret of the creative Word — “His command, when He wills a thing, is only to say to it: Be, and it is” — and upon the final return: all comes from the Hand that holds the dominion of all things, and to it all returns.
themes: the parable of the town and the man who came running
the creative Word and the return

versets.ar: يس
versets.tr: yā-sīn
versets.trad: Yā. Sīn.
versets.commentaire: It is said: yā-sīn means “O master.” It is also said: the yāʾ points to the Day of the primordial Covenant (yawm al-mīthāq), and the sīn to God's secret (sirr) with His beloved — as though He swore: by the right of the Day of the Covenant and by My secret with My beloved. Beneath two enigmatic letters lie hidden a name of the Prophet and an oath of love prior to time itself.

mots.def: opening letters; per Qushayrī, an allusion to the primordial Covenant and the divine secret.

versets.ar: وَالْقُرْآنِ الْحَكِيمِ
versets.tr: wa-l-qurʾāni l-ḥakīmi
versets.trad: By the wise Qurʾān!
versets.commentaire: God swears by the wise Qurʾān (al-ḥakīm), the Book of wisdom whose every verse is set down with perfect knowledge. The oath by the Word attests the truthfulness of the one who conveys it: the Qurʾān is itself the witness to the prophetic mission.

mots.def: qurʾān: the Recitation, the revealed Book.

versets.ar: إِنَّكَ لَمِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ
versets.tr: innaka la-mina l-mursalīna
versets.trad: Truly, you are among the messengers,
versets.commentaire: The answer to the oath: “Truly you — O Muḥammad — are indeed among the messengers.” What is sworn by the wise Qurʾān is the authenticity of the messenger: the Word and the bearer of the Word confirm one another.

versets.ar: عَلَىٰ صِرَاطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ
versets.tr: ʿalā ṣirāṭin mustaqīmin
versets.trad: upon a straight path.
versets.commentaire: And you are upon a straight path (ṣirāṭ mustaqīm): not seeking the way but embodying it. The Prophet's uprightness is his very station; he is himself the path that others follow toward God.

versets.ar: تَنزِيلَ الْعَزِيزِ الرَّحِيمِ
versets.tr: tanzīla l-ʿazīzi r-raḥīmi
versets.trad: a Revelation from the All-Mighty, the Compassionate,
versets.commentaire: This Book is the sending-down (tanzīl) of the Inaccessible (al-ʿAzīz): the Majestic who needs not the obedience of the obedient; the Compassionate (al-Raḥīm): He who lavishes His grace upon His believing servants. The same Word unites the power that suffices itself and the mercy that bends down.

versets.ar: لِتُنذِرَ قَوْمًا مَّا أُنذِرَ آبَاؤُهُمْ فَهُمْ غَافِلُونَ
versets.tr: li-tunḏira qawman mā unḏira ābāʾuhum fa-hum ghāfilūna
versets.trad: that you may warn a people whose forefathers were not warned, so they are heedless.
versets.commentaire: We singled you out by this Qurʾān, We sent down upon you this Criterion, that you might warn a people fallen into the time of interruption (the fatra), whose fathers passed away in that state of heedlessness. The messenger comes to fill a night in which no lamp was burning.

versets.ar: لَقَدْ حَقَّ الْقَوْلُ عَلَىٰ أَكْثَرِهِمْ فَهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: la-qad ḥaqqa l-qawlu ʿalā akṯarihim fa-hum lā yuʾminūna
versets.trad: The Word has come true against most of them, and they do not believe.
versets.commentaire: The sentence of punishment has proven true against most of them, for they persisted in their denial and sank deep into their ignorance. What is known of them and decreed upon them is that they will not believe — not that grace was denied them, but that they refused to reach out for it.

mots.racine: ح ق ق / ق و ل

versets.ar: إِنَّا جَعَلْنَا فِي أَعْنَاقِهِمْ أَغْلَالًا فَهِيَ إِلَى الْأَذْقَانِ فَهُم مُّقْمَحُونَ
versets.tr: innā jaʿalnā fī aʿnāqihim aghlālan fa-hiya ilā l-aḏqāni fa-hum muqmaḥūna
versets.trad: We have placed shackles upon their necks, reaching to the chins, so their heads are forced up.
versets.commentaire: The shackles that force up their heads are the image of their riveted pride: We shall drag them toward their abasement and make them taste the consequence of their affair. The stiffened neck that cannot bow figures the heart incapable of the humility that saves.

mots.def: muqmaḥ: with head raised and gaze constrained.

versets.ar: وَجَعَلْنَا مِن بَيْنِ أَيْدِيهِمْ سَدًّا وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِمْ سَدًّا فَأَغْشَيْنَاهُمْ فَهُمْ لَا يُبْصِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-jaʿalnā min bayni aydīhim saddan wa-min ḫalfihim saddan fa-aghshaynāhum fa-hum lā yubṣirūna
versets.trad: We have set a barrier before them and a barrier behind them, and We have covered them over, so they do not see.
versets.commentaire: We have drowned them today in the oceans of error and pitched around them the pavilions of ignorance; We have blinded them to the witnessing of the proof. A barrier before, a barrier behind: hemmed in on every side, the soul deprived of light no longer makes out the path of return.

versets.ar: وَسَوَاءٌ عَلَيْهِمْ أَأَنذَرْتَهُمْ أَمْ لَمْ تُنذِرْهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: wa-sawāʾun ʿalayhim a-anḏartahum am lam tunḏirhum lā yuʾminūna
versets.trad: It is the same to them whether you warn them or do not warn them: they will not believe.
versets.commentaire: Whomever the Truth has forsaken, none can reach; whomever the Truth has repelled, none can receive. The one whom the divine will has broken and the decree has fixed — counsel profits him not. The warning remains true, but it penetrates only the heart disposed to hear it.

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا تُنذِرُ مَنِ اتَّبَعَ الذِّكْرَ وَخَشِيَ الرَّحْمَٰنَ بِالْغَيْبِ ۖ فَبَشِّرْهُ بِمَغْفِرَةٍ وَأَجْرٍ كَرِيمٍ
versets.tr: innamā tunḏiru mani ttabaʿa ḏ-ḏikra wa-ḫashiya r-raḥmāna bi-l-ghaybi fa-bashshirhu bi-maghfiratin wa-ajrin karīmin
versets.trad: You only warn the one who follows the Reminder and fears the All-Merciful unseen. Give him glad tidings of forgiveness and a generous reward.
versets.commentaire: Your warning, though addressed to all, profits only the one who follows the Reminder. As for those who followed the Reminder, received clear sight, and drew fruit from what they heard of you, they deserve that you bring them glad tidings: bring them, then, and let the joy of your message appear upon them. The generous reward (ajr karīm) is ample and abundant over their deeds — even if some flaw be in them.

mots.racine: ت ب ع / ذ ك ر
خ ش ي / غ ي ب
mots.def: ḫashya: reverent awe; ghayb: the unseen.

mots.racine: أ ج ر / ك ر م

versets.ar: إِنَّا نَحْنُ نُحْيِ الْمَوْتَىٰ وَنَكْتُبُ مَا قَدَّمُوا وَآثَارَهُمْ ۚ وَكُلَّ شَيْءٍ أَحْصَيْنَاهُ فِي إِمَامٍ مُّبِينٍ
versets.tr: innā naḥnu nuḥyī l-mawtā wa-naktubu mā qaddamū wa-āṯārahum wa-kulla shayʾin aḥṣaynāhu fī imāmin mubīnin
versets.trad: Truly We give life to the dead, and We record what they have sent forth and their traces. Every thing We have counted in a clear register.
versets.commentaire: We give life to hearts that died of hardness by pouring upon them the rain of Our attention and nearness. And their traces (āṯār): their steps to the mosques, their standing on the carpet of intimate confiding, the trickling of their tears down their cheeks, the rising of their breaths. All this We have recorded — not for fear of forgetting it (how, when We have numbered all things?) but because We loved to inscribe the traces of Our beloved in the most secret part of Our Book.

mots.racine: ح ي ي / م و ت
mots.sens: We give life to the dead
mots.def: aḥyā: to give life; mawtā: the dead.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ق د م
mots.sens: We record what they sent forth
mots.def: kataba: to write; qaddama: to send ahead.

mots.racine: أ م م / ب ي ن

versets.ar: وَاضْرِبْ لَهُم مَّثَلًا أَصْحَابَ الْقَرْيَةِ إِذْ جَاءَهَا الْمُرْسَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ḍrib lahum maṯalan aṣḥāba l-qaryati iḏ jāʾahā l-mursalūna
versets.trad: Set forth to them a parable: the people of the town, when the messengers came to it,
versets.commentaire: Their time has passed, their age and affair forgotten! Yet We remember their states after their hours have fled, and We are not content that the mention of the absent and the bygone should cease to circulate among Our beloved and upon the tongues of those near to Us. So the parable of the town revives, for the living, the memory of those who once believed.

mots.racine: ض ر ب / م ث ل
mots.def: ḍaraba maṯalan: to give an example.

mots.racine: ص ح ب / ق ر ي

versets.ar: إِذْ أَرْسَلْنَا إِلَيْهِمُ اثْنَيْنِ فَكَذَّبُوهُمَا فَعَزَّزْنَا بِثَالِثٍ فَقَالُوا إِنَّا إِلَيْكُم مُّرْسَلُونَ
versets.tr: iḏ arsalnā ilayhimu ṯnayni fa-kaḏḏabūhumā fa-ʿazzaznā bi-ṯāliṯin fa-qālū innā ilaykum mursalūna
versets.trad: When We sent to them two messengers, they called them liars; so We strengthened them with a third, and they said: “Truly we are sent to you!”
versets.commentaire: Two messengers denied, then reinforced by a third: God does not leave His calls without support; when stubbornness redoubles, grace redoubles the reminder. The messengers do not weary of refusal; they repeat: “Truly we are sent to you,” for fidelity to the message does not depend on its reception.

mots.racine: ر س ل / ث ن ي
ع ز ز / ث ل ث

versets.ar: قَالُوا مَا أَنتُمْ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُنَا وَمَا أَنزَلَ الرَّحْمَٰنُ مِن شَيْءٍ إِنْ أَنتُمْ إِلَّا تَكْذِبُونَ
versets.tr: qālū mā antum illā basharun miṯlunā wa-mā anzala r-raḥmānu min shayʾin in antum illā takḏibūna
versets.trad: They said: “You are only mortals like us; the All-Merciful has sent down nothing at all, and you do nothing but lie!”
versets.commentaire: They confront the messengers with their humanity: “You are only mortals like us.” Such is the blindness that measures the messenger by his flesh and fails to see the light he bears. To refuse that God should speak through a man is to refuse the very way by which He chose to make Himself known.

mots.racine: ب ش ر / م ث ل
ن ز ل / ر ح م
mots.sens: the All-Merciful has sent down nothing

versets.ar: قَالُوا رَبُّنَا يَعْلَمُ إِنَّا إِلَيْكُمْ لَمُرْسَلُونَ
versets.tr: qālū rabbunā yaʿlamu innā ilaykum la-mursalūna
versets.trad: They said: “Our Lord knows that we are indeed sent to you;
versets.commentaire: The messengers replied: “Our Lord knows that we are indeed sent to you.” When the denial of creatures presses upon them, they appeal only to God's testimony: for whomever God suffices as witness, the denial of men weighs nothing.

mots.racine: ر ب ب / ع ل م

versets.ar: وَمَا عَلَيْنَا إِلَّا الْبَلَاغُ الْمُبِينُ
versets.tr: wa-mā ʿalaynā illā l-balāghu l-mubīnu
versets.trad: and nothing is upon us but the clear delivery of the message.”
versets.commentaire: Our knowledge bears only on what we were commanded: to convey and to warn. The messenger's charge ends at the clarity of the message (al-balāgh al-mubīn); the outcome belongs to God alone. To deliver faithfully, without coercing hearts: such is the limit and the nobility of the mission.

mots.racine: ب ل غ / ب ي ن

versets.ar: قَالُوا إِنَّا تَطَيَّرْنَا بِكُمْ ۖ لَئِن لَّمْ تَنتَهُوا لَنَرْجُمَنَّكُمْ وَلَيَمَسَّنَّكُم مِّنَّا عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: qālū innā taṭayyarnā bikum la-in lam tantahū la-narjumannakum wa-la-yamassannakum minnā ʿaḏābun alīmun
versets.trad: They said: “We see in you an evil omen; if you do not desist, we will surely stone you, and a painful punishment will touch you from us.”
versets.commentaire: They took the messengers for a foul omen and threatened to stone them. Such is the way of the closed heart: it imputes to the bearers of light the misfortune born of its own refusal. What they call an evil omen is only the reflection of their inner darkness cast upon those who summon them.

mots.sens: we see an evil omen in you
mots.def: taṭayyara: to take as a bad omen.

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / أ ل م

versets.ar: قَالُوا طَائِرُكُم مَّعَكُمْ ۚ أَئِن ذُكِّرْتُم ۚ بَلْ أَنتُمْ قَوْمٌ مُّسْرِفُونَ
versets.tr: qālū ṭāʾirukum maʿakum a-in ḏukkirtum bal antum qawmun musrifūna
versets.trad: They said: “Your omen is with yourselves. Is it because you were reminded? Nay, you are a transgressing people.”
versets.commentaire: The messengers answered them: through your ignorance and your denial, you will meet what you are threatened with. Your omen is within yourselves: it is not the reminder that brings misfortune but the heart that turns from it. Evil comes not from the light offered, but from the eye that refuses to receive it.

versets.ar: وَجَاءَ مِنْ أَقْصَى الْمَدِينَةِ رَجُلٌ يَسْعَىٰ قَالَ يَا قَوْمِ اتَّبِعُوا الْمُرْسَلِينَ
versets.tr: wa-jāʾa min aqṣā l-madīnati rajulun yasʿā qāla yā qawmi ttabiʿū l-mursalīna
versets.trad: And a man came running from the far end of the city. He said: “O my people! Follow the messengers!
versets.commentaire: The account says he came “from the farthest part of the city” — not that its near and far quarters differed much, but God — glory be to Him — is wont to magnify the little of His servant's deed when it pleases Him, and to count as scant the abundance of His own favor when He bestows it. This man, running from afar in his eager haste, gives the example of the soul that hurries toward truth the moment it hears it.

mots.racine: ق ص و / م د ن
mots.sens: the far end of the city

mots.racine: ر ج ل / س ع ي

versets.ar: اتَّبِعُوا مَن لَّا يَسْأَلُكُمْ أَجْرًا وَهُم مُّهْتَدُونَ
versets.tr: ittabiʿū man lā yasʾalukum ajran wa-hum muhtadūna
versets.trad: Follow those who ask of you no reward and who are rightly guided.
versets.commentaire: “Follow those who ask of you no reward”: he gave the best of counsel and the most sincere admonition. The sign of the true guide is that he covets nothing from those he summons: his word is pure gift, and by this disinterestedness it is known that he is himself guided.

mots.racine: س أ ل / أ ج ر

versets.ar: وَمَا لِيَ لَا أَعْبُدُ الَّذِي فَطَرَنِي وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā liya lā aʿbudu llaḏī faṭaranī wa-ilayhi turjaʿūna
versets.trad: Why should I not worship the One who created me, and to whom you will be returned?
versets.commentaire: “Why should I not worship the One who fashioned me?” The servant's primordial nature (fiṭra) inclines him toward his Creator; to worship Him is no constraint but the return of water to its source. And “to Him you will be returned”: the end rejoins the beginning, for He who brought being forth is also He toward whom all things flow back.

versets.ar: أَأَتَّخِذُ مِن دُونِهِ آلِهَةً إِن يُرِدْنِ الرَّحْمَٰنُ بِضُرٍّ لَّا تُغْنِ عَنِّي شَفَاعَتُهُمْ شَيْئًا وَلَا يُنقِذُونِ
versets.tr: a-attaḫiḏu min dūnihi ālihatan in yuridni r-raḥmānu bi-ḍurrin lā tughni ʿannī shafāʿatuhum shayʾan wa-lā yunqiḏūni
versets.trad: Shall I take besides Him gods whose intercession, if the All-Merciful should intend me harm, would avail me nothing, nor could they save me?
versets.commentaire: “Shall I take, besides Him, gods?” One who has tasted that all is in the hand of the All-Merciful cannot turn to what has no power to harm or to save. Idols neither intercede nor rescue: to trust in them is to lean upon the nonexistent against the One who holds existence itself.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / أ ل ه

versets.ar: إِنِّي إِذًا لَّفِي ضَلَالٍ مُّبِينٍ
versets.tr: innī iḏan la-fī ḍalālin mubīnin
versets.trad: Then I would surely be in manifest error.
versets.commentaire: “Then I would surely be in manifest error.” To leave the worship of the One for the multiplicity of idols is to exchange the light of evidence for the night of error. The believer names plainly what he flees: the worst of errors is to serve what can do nothing.

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ب ي ن

versets.ar: إِنِّي آمَنتُ بِرَبِّكُمْ فَاسْمَعُونِ
versets.tr: innī āmantu bi-rabbikum fa-smaʿūni
versets.trad: Truly I have believed in your Lord, so listen to me!”
versets.commentaire: “Truly I have believed in your Lord, so listen to me!” When he was sincere in his state, endured what he suffered from his people, and turned back to God in repentance, God received him with the beauty of His bounty and gathered him into the shade of His nearness. To proclaim one's faith before those who threaten is to seal the gift of the soul with the courage of the witness.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ر ب ب
mots.sens: I have believed in your Lord

versets.ar: قِيلَ ادْخُلِ الْجَنَّةَ ۖ قَالَ يَا لَيْتَ قَوْمِي يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: qīla dḫuli l-jannata qāla yā layta qawmī yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: It was said: “Enter the Garden.” He said: “Would that my people knew
versets.commentaire: He wished that his people might know his state; God fulfilled his wish, made his condition known, addressed His Word to him, and revealed it to his people. He formed this wish only out of compassion for them, that they might act as he had acted to find what he had found. Such is the martyr after God's own heart: at the very moment he enters the Garden, he thinks only of the salvation of those who rejected him.

mots.racine: د خ ل / ج ن ن
mots.def: daḫala: to enter; janna: garden, paradise.

mots.racine: ق و م / ع ل م

versets.ar: بِمَا غَفَرَ لِي رَبِّي وَجَعَلَنِي مِنَ الْمُكْرَمِينَ
versets.tr: bimā ghafara lī rabbī wa-jaʿalanī mina l-mukramīna
versets.trad: how my Lord has forgiven me and placed me among the honored!”
versets.commentaire: “...how my Lord has forgiven me and set me among the honored.” His wish that his people learn of his lot sprang from tenderness: that they might work as he did to find what he found. The honored one (mukram) does not keep the sweetness of pardon for himself alone; he would have all those he loved enter into it.

mots.racine: غ ف ر / ر ب ب

versets.ar: وَمَا أَنزَلْنَا عَلَىٰ قَوْمِهِ مِن بَعْدِهِ مِن جُندٍ مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ وَمَا كُنَّا مُنزِلِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mā anzalnā ʿalā qawmihi min baʿdihi min jundin mina s-samāʾi wa-mā kunnā munzilīna
versets.trad: We did not send down upon his people after him any host from the sky, nor would We send one down.
versets.commentaire: There was here only a decree from Us to punish them, a turning of the state of safety they were in into the state of affliction. No celestial armies are needed to destroy the wrongdoer: He whose command is “Be!” has no recourse to deploying His legions.

mots.racine: ج ن د / س م و

versets.ar: إِن كَانَتْ إِلَّا صَيْحَةً وَاحِدَةً فَإِذَا هُمْ خَامِدُونَ
versets.tr: in kānat illā ṣayḥatan wāḥidatan fa-iḏā hum ḫāmidūna
versets.trad: It was but a single cry, and behold, they were extinguished.
versets.commentaire: A single cry, and there they are, snuffed out like embers. Omnipotence is not measured by the scale of its means: one breath suffices to extinguish what pride thought unshakable. Thus collapses, in an instant, all that man has built against the truth.

mots.racine: ص ي ح / و ح د

versets.ar: يَا حَسْرَةً عَلَى الْعِبَادِ ۚ مَا يَأْتِيهِم مِّن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا كَانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِئُونَ
versets.tr: yā ḥasratan ʿalā l-ʿibādi mā yaʾtīhim min rasūlin illā kānū bihi yastahziʾūna
versets.trad: Oh, what sorrow over the servants! Never did a messenger come to them but they mocked him.
versets.commentaire: If they do not grieve today, they have their place of grieving reserved — and that for having strung themselves on the single thread of denial, opposition to the messengers, and hostility to God's friends. Mockery of the messengers is the seal of the heart that deprives itself of the light held out to it.

versets.ar: أَلَمْ يَرَوْا كَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا قَبْلَهُم مِّنَ الْقُرُونِ أَنَّهُمْ إِلَيْهِمْ لَا يَرْجِعُونَ
versets.tr: a-lam yaraw kam ahlaknā qablahum mina l-qurūni annahum ilayhim lā yarjiʿūna
versets.trad: Do they not see how many generations We destroyed before them — never to return to them again,
versets.commentaire: Do they not see what We did with the generations before them, how We dealt with the bygone nations? Not one of them returned: all are in the grip of Power. None escaped Us, none had any help against Us, none found refuge from Our decree. The effaced trace of ancient peoples is a mirror held up to the living.

versets.ar: وَإِن كُلٌّ لَّمَّا جَمِيعٌ لَّدَيْنَا مُحْضَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-in kullun lammā jamīʿun ladaynā muḥḍarūna
versets.trad: yet all of them, together, will be brought before Us.
versets.commentaire: All, without exception, will be brought and presented before Us. The past is not oblivion but waiting: what the earth has covered, the decree will bring back for the gathering. No one is so scattered as to escape the summons of Him who has numbered all things.

versets.ar: وَآيَةٌ لَّهُمُ الْأَرْضُ الْمَيْتَةُ أَحْيَيْنَاهَا وَأَخْرَجْنَا مِنْهَا حَبًّا فَمِنْهُ يَأْكُلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-āyatun lahumu l-arḍu l-maytatu aḥyaynāhā wa-aḫrajnā minhā ḥabban fa-minhu yaʾkulūna
versets.trad: A sign for them is the dead earth We bring to life and from which We bring forth grain, of which they eat.
themes: the sign of the dead earth
versets.commentaire: Since the matter of resurrection was their greatest objection and the seat of their denial, God repeated its reminder often and set forth its image through the reviving of the earth. The dead earth made green again by the rain is the tangible proof offered to whoever doubts the return: He who revives the buried grain will revive the buried man. And it is also the hardened heart that His grace makes green again.

mots.racine: أ ر ض / م و ت

versets.ar: وَجَعَلْنَا فِيهَا جَنَّاتٍ مِّن نَّخِيلٍ وَأَعْنَابٍ وَفَجَّرْنَا فِيهَا مِنَ الْعُيُونِ
versets.tr: wa-jaʿalnā fīhā jannātin min naḫīlin wa-aʿnābin wa-fajjarnā fīhā mina l-ʿuyūni
versets.trad: We placed in it gardens of date-palms and grapevines, and We made springs gush forth in it,
versets.commentaire: Gardens of palms and vines, gushing springs: so many tables spread by divine generosity for whoever can read, in the visible gift, the hand of the invisible Giver. The orchard is not only nourishment for the body; it is a sign, for the attentive heart, of the bounty of Him who makes water well up from the parched ground.

mots.racine: ن خ ل / ع ن ب

versets.ar: لِيَأْكُلُوا مِن ثَمَرِهِ وَمَا عَمِلَتْهُ أَيْدِيهِمْ ۖ أَفَلَا يَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: li-yaʾkulū min ṯamarihi wa-mā ʿamilathu aydīhim a-fa-lā yashkurūna
versets.trad: that they may eat of its fruit — though their hands did not make it. Will they not give thanks?
versets.commentaire: “That they may eat of its fruit — which their hands did not fashion. Will they not give thanks?” The fruit ripens by a power no human hand produces; to recognize this is to pass from the gift to the Giver. Ingratitude consists in enjoying the bounty while forgetting the One who makes it grow.

mots.racine: ع م ل / ي د ي

versets.ar: سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ الْأَزْوَاجَ كُلَّهَا مِمَّا تُنبِتُ الْأَرْضُ وَمِنْ أَنفُسِهِمْ وَمِمَّا لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: subḥāna llaḏī ḫalaqa l-azwāja kullahā mimmā tunbitu l-arḍu wa-min anfusihim wa-mimmā lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: Glory to Him who created all the pairs — of what the earth grows, of themselves, and of what they do not know.
versets.commentaire: This verse awakens reflection on the wonder of His handiwork. Glory to Him who created, in matched pairs, the plant, the human, and so many realities whose detail men do not know! Consider how He diversified tastes and scents, forms and figures, the foliage of trees, their branches and trunks, their flowers and fruits — and the use bound to each. He whose eye God has lined with the kohl of blessed knowing, and whose sight He has perfected so that no veil halts it — how strong is his proof, how clear his way!

mots.racine: ز و ج / ك ل ل
ن ب ت / أ ر ض

versets.ar: وَآيَةٌ لَّهُمُ اللَّيْلُ نَسْلَخُ مِنْهُ النَّهَارَ فَإِذَا هُم مُّظْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-āyatun lahumu l-laylu naslaḫu minhu n-nahāra fa-iḏā hum muẓlimūna
versets.trad: A sign for them is the night, from which We strip away the day, and behold, they are in darkness,
versets.commentaire: We abolish the brightness of the day by the onset of night, and We dispel the darkness of night by the onset of day; so too the day of existence enters upon the nights of standstill, and God leads, by the hand of His generosity, the one blind to his own rightness, guiding him to the level road. The drawing of day out of night is the image of the unveiling that strips the heart of its veils.

versets.ar: وَالشَّمْسُ تَجْرِي لِمُسْتَقَرٍّ لَّهَا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ تَقْدِيرُ الْعَزِيزِ الْعَلِيمِ
versets.tr: wa-sh-shamsu tajrī li-mustaqarrin lahā ḏālika taqdīru l-ʿazīzi l-ʿalīmi
versets.trad: and the sun runs to its appointed resting-place — that is the decree of the All-Mighty, the All-Knowing,
versets.commentaire: According to a known order that does not vary through the seasons: each day the sun has a new rising and a new setting — and all this by the decree of the Inaccessible, the All-Knowing. The star faithful to its orbit teaches the servant the beauty of constancy: to move, without deviating, in the way that Wisdom has traced for it.

mots.racine: ش م س / ج ر ي

versets.ar: وَالْقَمَرَ قَدَّرْنَاهُ مَنَازِلَ حَتَّىٰ عَادَ كَالْعُرْجُونِ الْقَدِيمِ
versets.tr: wa-l-qamara qaddarnāhu manāzila ḥattā ʿāda ka-l-ʿurjūni l-qadīmi
versets.trad: and the moon — We have measured for it stations, until it returns like an old withered palm-stalk.
versets.commentaire: The allusion here is that the servant, in the time of his seeking, is of feeble state, weak, of scant understanding; then he reflects until his clear-sight grows — he is like the moon that becomes full, then wanes, drawing little by little nearer to the sun; and the closer it comes to the sun, the more it wanes in itself until it vanishes and disappears. Such is the moon of changing states (talwīn); but the sun figures the established knower (tamkīn) who shines without eclipse.

mots.racine: ق د ر / ن ز ل
ع ر ج ن / ق د م

versets.ar: لَا الشَّمْسُ يَنبَغِي لَهَا أَن تُدْرِكَ الْقَمَرَ وَلَا اللَّيْلُ سَابِقُ النَّهَارِ ۚ وَكُلٌّ فِي فَلَكٍ يَسْبَحُونَ
versets.tr: lā sh-shamsu yanbaghī lahā an tudrika l-qamara wa-lā l-laylu sābiqu n-nahāri wa-kullun fī falakin yasbaḥūna
versets.trad: It does not befit the sun to overtake the moon, nor for the night to outstrip the day: each floats in an orbit.
versets.commentaire: The sun does not overtake the moon, the night does not outstrip the day: each floats in its sphere. Like the sun is the knower forever in the light of his knowing, endowed with stability (tamkīn) without change of color, ever rising from the citadel of his felicity, whom no eclipse reaches and no cloud veils. Like the moon is the servant whose states vary (talwīn): now expanded, he rises to the threshold of union; then brought back to languor, he falls into contraction and wanes, until the Real graces him to return from his torpor and recover from his intoxication. Each star, faithful to its orbit, declares the faithfulness of the decree.

mots.racine: د ر ك / ق م ر
س ب ق / ن ه ر
ف ل ك / س ب ح
mots.def: falak: sphere, orbit; sabaḥa: to float, swim.

versets.ar: وَآيَةٌ لَّهُمْ أَنَّا حَمَلْنَا ذُرِّيَّتَهُمْ فِي الْفُلْكِ الْمَشْحُونِ
versets.tr: wa-āyatun lahum annā ḥamalnā ḏurriyyatahum fī l-fulki l-mashḥūni
versets.trad: A sign for them is that We carried their offspring in the laden ship,
versets.commentaire: The allusion is to the carrying of creatures in the ship of safety, upon the oceans of the decree, when the waves of upheavals and trials surge. How many servants drown in the bustle of their days and nights, with not a moment's rest, until they forget their final end! But when God keeps a servant in the ship of His solicitude, He frees him from bondage to base things and grants him, in his inmost depths, the freedom of a heart turned toward its Lord.

mots.racine: ف ل ك / ش ح ن

versets.ar: وَخَلَقْنَا لَهُم مِّن مِّثْلِهِ مَا يَرْكَبُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ḫalaqnā lahum min miṯlihi mā yarkabūna
versets.trad: and We created for them the like of it, that they ride upon.
versets.commentaire: And We created for them other like vessels. Just as God prepares for the body ships to cross the seas, He prepares for the heart secret means to cross the ocean of this life without drowning in it. Every ease granted to the servant is a ship of providence whose builder must be recognized.

versets.ar: وَإِن نَّشَأْ نُغْرِقْهُمْ فَلَا صَرِيخَ لَهُمْ وَلَا هُمْ يُنقَذُونَ
versets.tr: wa-in nashaʾ nughriqhum fa-lā ṣarīḫa lahum wa-lā hum yunqaḏūna
versets.trad: If We willed, We could drown them, and they would have no rescuer, nor would they be saved,
versets.commentaire: If We willed, We could drown them, with no rescuer and no savior. The ship that carries, carries only by the will of Him who can submerge it. The lucid servant never claims his own salvation: at every moment he knows that one divine will alone holds him above the abyss.

mots.def: ṣarīḫ: one who answers the cry for help.

versets.ar: إِلَّا رَحْمَةً مِّنَّا وَمَتَاعًا إِلَىٰ حِينٍ
versets.tr: illā raḥmatan minnā wa-matāʿan ilā ḥīnin
versets.trad: except by a mercy from Us and an enjoyment for a while.
versets.commentaire: Were it not for His generosity and favor, the same misfortune that struck their like would have befallen them; but through the beauty of His kindness, He preserves them in all their states. The respite granted is but a mercy and an enjoyment “for a while”: the believer uses it as a loan, knowing that grace alone, not his merit, keeps him alive.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمُ اتَّقُوا مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَمَا خَلْفَكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-iḏā qīla lahumu ttaqū mā bayna aydīkum wa-mā ḫalfakum laʿallakum turḥamūna
versets.trad: When it is said to them: “Beware of what is before you and what is behind you, that you may receive mercy”…
versets.commentaire: Such are the marks of those whom God has left to wander in the valleys of abandonment, branded with the seal of deprivation, made deaf to the hearing of rightness. They are bidden to fear what lies before and behind them, in hope of mercy — but their hearts, closed in advance, do not receive the call. Saving fear presupposes a heart still able to be moved.

versets.ar: وَمَا تَأْتِيهِم مِّنْ آيَةٍ مِّنْ آيَاتِ رَبِّهِمْ إِلَّا كَانُوا عَنْهَا مُعْرِضِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mā taʾtīhim min āyatin min āyāti rabbihim illā kānū ʿanhā muʿriḍīna
versets.trad: No sign comes to them among the signs of their Lord but they turn away from it;
versets.commentaire: No sign comes to them as a warning but they meet it with aversion, turning from the lesson in their perpetual recoil. Signs abound, yet the eye that closes receives nothing of them: it is not the light that is lacking, but the gaze that withdraws.

mots.racine: أ ي ي / ر ب ب

versets.ar: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ أَنفِقُوا مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ اللَّهُ قَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَنُطْعِمُ مَن لَّوْ يَشَاءُ اللَّهُ أَطْعَمَهُ إِنْ أَنتُمْ إِلَّا فِي ضَلَالٍ مُّبِينٍ
versets.tr: wa-iḏā qīla lahum anfiqū mimmā razaqakumu llāhu qāla llaḏīna kafarū li-llaḏīna āmanū a-nuṭʿimu man law yashāʾu llāhu aṭʿamahu in antum illā fī ḍalālin mubīnin
versets.trad: And when it is said to them: “Spend of what God has provided you,” the disbelievers say to the believers: “Shall we feed one whom God could feed if He willed? You are clearly in error!”
versets.commentaire: When they are commanded to spend and to feed the poor, they object that God is the Provider of creatures and that, if He willed, He would provide for them Himself. A sophism of avarice: making the divine decree a pretext to refuse sharing. But it is precisely through the believer's hand that God chooses to feed: to refuse to give is to cut oneself off from the channel of His generosity.

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ب ي ن

versets.ar: وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هَٰذَا الْوَعْدُ إِن كُنتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-yaqūlūna matā hāḏā l-waʿdu in kuntum ṣādiqīna
versets.trad: And they say: “When will this promise come, if you are truthful?”
versets.commentaire: They press for the coming of the Hour and find the Resurrection slow — not out of a faith that would deliver them from doubt, nor a fear that would turn them from error, but out of denial of the messengers' call, rejection of the truth of prophecy, and disbelief in the gathering. To demand the proof in mockery is to ask for light while keeping the eyes shut.

versets.ar: مَا يَنظُرُونَ إِلَّا صَيْحَةً وَاحِدَةً تَأْخُذُهُمْ وَهُمْ يَخِصِّمُونَ
versets.tr: mā yanẓurūna illā ṣayḥatan wāḥidatan taʾḫuḏuhum wa-hum yaḫiṣṣimūna
versets.trad: They await but a single cry that will seize them while they wrangle.
versets.commentaire: A single cry will seize them in the midst of their disputes. The Hour surprises man absorbed in his quarrels and reckonings, just where he thought himself most secure. While they argue over the delay of the promise, the promise falls upon them: none has the leisure to defer what seizes him without warning.

mots.racine: ص ي ح / و ح د

versets.ar: فَلَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ تَوْصِيَةً وَلَا إِلَىٰ أَهْلِهِمْ يَرْجِعُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lā yastaṭīʿūna tawṣiyatan wa-lā ilā ahlihim yarjiʿūna
versets.trad: Then they will be unable to make a bequest, nor return to their families.
versets.commentaire: They will be unable to make a will or to return to their kin. The instant of the seizing leaves no delay to repair what one has neglected. The wise do not wait for that cry to set their hearts in order: they live each hour as if, at the next, they must appear without recourse.

versets.ar: وَنُفِخَ فِي الصُّورِ فَإِذَا هُم مِّنَ الْأَجْدَاثِ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ يَنسِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-nufiḫa fī ṣ-ṣūri fa-iḏā hum mina l-ajdāṯi ilā rabbihim yansilūna
versets.trad: The trumpet will be blown, and behold, from the graves they will hasten to their Lord.
versets.commentaire: At the blast of the trumpet, behold them sprung from the graves, racing to their Lord. They die by compulsion, are gathered by compulsion, receive the command by compulsion, and possess for themselves neither benefit nor harm. The race toward the Lord is then no longer a choice but an evidence: all returns to its source, whether it willed it or not.

mots.racine: ن ف خ / ص و ر

versets.ar: قَالُوا يَا وَيْلَنَا مَن بَعَثَنَا مِن مَّرْقَدِنَا ۜ ۗ هَٰذَا مَا وَعَدَ الرَّحْمَٰنُ وَصَدَقَ الْمُرْسَلُونَ
versets.tr: qālū yā waylanā man baʿaṯanā min marqadinā hāḏā mā waʿada r-raḥmānu wa-ṣadaqa l-mursalūna
versets.trad: They will say: “Woe to us! Who has raised us from our resting-place? This is what the All-Merciful promised, and the messengers spoke the truth!”
versets.commentaire: “Woe to us! Who has roused us from our bed?” They died in ignorance, not knowing their Lord; they rise in the same state, not knowing who raised them. They counted the severe punishment of their grave — beside the new torments awaiting them — as a sleep and a repose. Too late they confess the truth of the promise and the truthfulness of the messengers: the confession without faith no longer saves.

mots.racine: ص د ق / ر س ل

versets.ar: إِن كَانَتْ إِلَّا صَيْحَةً وَاحِدَةً فَإِذَا هُمْ جَمِيعٌ لَّدَيْنَا مُحْضَرُونَ
versets.tr: in kānat illā ṣayḥatan wāḥidatan fa-iḏā hum jamīʿun ladaynā muḥḍarūna
versets.trad: It will be but a single cry, and behold, they will all be brought before Us.
versets.commentaire: A single cry, and they are all presented before Us. What to denial seemed a “far return” is accomplished in an instant. The same Power that with a cry extinguished the peoples, with a cry gathers them: the beginning and the end obey the same creative Word.

mots.racine: ص ي ح / و ح د

versets.ar: فَالْيَوْمَ لَا تُظْلَمُ نَفْسٌ شَيْئًا وَلَا تُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا مَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: fa-l-yawma lā tuẓlamu nafsun shayʾan wa-lā tujzawna illā mā kuntum taʿmalūna
versets.trad: On that Day no soul will be wronged in anything, and you will be repaid only for what you used to do.
versets.commentaire: On that Day no soul is wronged, none is repaid but according to his deed. The divine balance knows neither excess nor deficiency: each reaps exactly what he sowed. This flawless justice is a veiled mercy: it ensures that nothing of the good done, however small, will be lost.

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ن ف س

versets.ar: إِنَّ أَصْحَابَ الْجَنَّةِ الْيَوْمَ فِي شُغُلٍ فَاكِهُونَ
versets.tr: inna aṣḥāba l-jannati l-yawma fī shughulin fākihūna
versets.trad: Truly, the people of the Garden are this Day in joyful occupation,
versets.commentaire: The servant is named after whatever dominated his remembrance and held his heart: the man of this world is captive to this world, and the people of the Garden are those who sought it, worked for it, and labored to attain it. Yet these states, however lofty, fall short of those of the great masters: whoever was, here below, free of the world may well, in Paradise, remain free even of Paradise — his occupation being only the contemplation of the Giver.

mots.racine: ص ح ب / ج ن ن

versets.ar: هُمْ وَأَزْوَاجُهُمْ فِي ظِلَالٍ عَلَى الْأَرَائِكِ مُتَّكِئُونَ
versets.tr: hum wa-azwājuhum fī ẓilālin ʿalā l-arāʾiki muttakiʾūna
versets.trad: they and their spouses, in shade, reclining upon couches.
versets.commentaire: They and their spouses, in the shade, reclining upon couches. This fulfilled repose is the exact opposite of the damned's agitation: the breathless running of this world gives way to the stillness of one who has found his Lord. The shade of the Garden is the image of the peace of the soul at last established beside the Beloved.

versets.ar: لَهُمْ فِيهَا فَاكِهَةٌ وَلَهُم مَّا يَدَّعُونَ
versets.tr: lahum fīhā fākihatun wa-lahum mā yaddaʿūna
versets.trad: For them therein is fruit, and for them whatever they call for.
versets.commentaire: For them there is fruit, and whatever they ask for. In the Garden, desire is no longer lack but immediate fulfillment: scarcely wished for, the good is given. Yet the greatest of gifts remains, for the knower, beyond fruits and delights: the good pleasure of the Lord and the nearness of His face.

versets.ar: سَلَامٌ قَوْلًا مِّن رَّبٍّ رَّحِيمٍ
versets.tr: salāmun qawlan min rabbin raḥīmin
versets.trad: “Peace!” — a word from a Compassionate Lord.
versets.commentaire: “Peace!” — a word from a Compassionate Lord. Of all the delights of the Garden, the highest is this word addressed directly by the Lord: the divine greeting that seals union and stills all desire. No gift equals the Word of the Beloved Himself; in this salām bliss culminates, for it is nearness that gives itself there.

mots.racine: ر ب ب / ر ح م

versets.ar: وَامْتَازُوا الْيَوْمَ أَيُّهَا الْمُجْرِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mtāzū l-yawma ayyuhā l-mujrimūna
versets.trad: But stand apart this Day, O you criminals!
versets.commentaire: “Stand apart, O criminals!” The Day of separation distinguishes what this world held mingled: souls sort themselves according to their innermost secret. To be set apart is to see openly revealed the choice one made in the shadows; the outward separation is only the manifestation of an estrangement already consummated in the heart.

mots.def: imtāza: to be distinguished, set apart.

versets.ar: أَلَمْ أَعْهَدْ إِلَيْكُمْ يَا بَنِي آدَمَ أَن لَّا تَعْبُدُوا الشَّيْطَانَ ۖ إِنَّهُ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: a-lam aʿhad ilaykum yā banī ādama an lā taʿbudū sh-shayṭāna innahu lakum ʿaduwwun mubīnun
versets.trad: Did I not enjoin upon you, O children of Adam, that you not worship Satan — he is to you a manifest enemy —
versets.commentaire: “Did I not make a covenant with you, O children of Adam, not to worship Satan?” The reminder recalls the primordial pact inscribed in man's very nature: to serve God alone. To worship the devil is not to prostrate before him, but to obey his suggestion; and to obey the declared enemy is to betray the pledge the soul had sealed before time.

mots.def: ʿahida: to make a pact, enjoin.

mots.racine: ع ب د / ش ط ن
ع د و / ب ي ن

versets.ar: وَأَنِ اعْبُدُونِي ۚ هَٰذَا صِرَاطٌ مُّسْتَقِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-ani ʿbudūnī hāḏā ṣirāṭun mustaqīmun
versets.trad: and that you worship Me? This is a straight path.
versets.commentaire: “...and that you worship Me: this is a straight path.” Uprightness is no external rule but the orientation of one's whole being toward the One. To worship God alone is to walk the ṣirāṭ mustaqīm: not one path among others, but the very axis that links the heart to its source and brings it back whence it came.

mots.racine: ص ر ط / ق و م

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ أَضَلَّ مِنكُمْ جِبِلًّا كَثِيرًا ۖ أَفَلَمْ تَكُونُوا تَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-la-qad aḍalla minkum jibillan kaṯīran a-fa-lam takūnū taʿqilūna
versets.trad: Yet he has led astray a great many of you. Did you not then understand?
versets.commentaire: “He has led astray many of you: did you not reason?” Intelligence (ʿaql) is the gift by which man discerns the enemy beneath the disguise of the friend. So many misguided generations should have served as a lesson; not to reason is to leave dormant the very light God placed in us to unmask the ruse.

mots.racine: ج ب ل / ك ث ر

versets.ar: هَٰذِهِ جَهَنَّمُ الَّتِي كُنتُمْ تُوعَدُونَ
versets.tr: hāḏihi jahannamu llatī kuntum tūʿadūna
versets.trad: This is the Hell you were promised:
versets.commentaire: “This is the Hell you were promised.” What was held to be distant and improbable now stands before the eyes. Denial only postponed the reckoning without erasing it; the promise, whether it announces grace or punishment, is always fulfilled, for the Word of God never returns empty.

versets.ar: اصْلَوْهَا الْيَوْمَ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَكْفُرُونَ
versets.tr: iṣlawhā l-yawma bimā kuntum takfurūna
versets.trad: burn in it this Day for what you used to deny.
versets.commentaire: “Burn in it this Day for your denial.” The fire is no arbitrary punishment but the very fruit of what they chose: denial smoldered in them like an ember, it is now revealed as flame. Every act bears its consequence within it; the Day only unveils what the heart had kindled.

mots.sens: for what you used to deny

versets.ar: الْيَوْمَ نَخْتِمُ عَلَىٰ أَفْوَاهِهِمْ وَتُكَلِّمُنَا أَيْدِيهِمْ وَتَشْهَدُ أَرْجُلُهُم بِمَا كَانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ
versets.tr: al-yawma naḫtimu ʿalā afwāhihim wa-tukallimunā aydīhim wa-tashhadu arjuluhum bimā kānū yaksibūna
versets.trad: This Day We seal their mouths, and their hands speak to Us, and their feet bear witness to what they earned.
versets.commentaire: The Day We seal the mouths and the limbs themselves speak: the tongue that lied is reduced to silence, and the mute flesh becomes a witness. The whole being of man gives account; nothing done by hands and feet escapes confession. The lucid servant lives, even here below, in the awareness that his limbs are writing upon him a testimony he will not be able to refute.

mots.racine: خ ت م / ف و ه
ك ل م / ي د ي
ش ه د / ر ج ل

versets.ar: وَلَوْ نَشَاءُ لَطَمَسْنَا عَلَىٰ أَعْيُنِهِمْ فَاسْتَبَقُوا الصِّرَاطَ فَأَنَّىٰ يُبْصِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-law nashāʾu la-ṭamasnā ʿalā aʿyunihim fa-stabaqū ṣ-ṣirāṭa fa-annā yubṣirūna
versets.trad: Had We willed, We could have blotted out their eyes; then they would race for the path — but how could they see?
versets.commentaire: “Had We willed, We could have effaced their eyes.” Outward blindness is but the image of a deeper blindness: that of the heart which, having refused the light offered, can no longer make out the way. How many race along a path they cannot see! True insight (baṣīra) lies not in the eye but in the heart that God illumines.

mots.racine: ط م س / ع ي ن
mots.sens: We would blot out their eyes
mots.def: ṭamasa: to efface, obliterate; ʿayn: eye.

mots.racine: س ب ق / ص ر ط
mots.sens: they would race for the path

versets.ar: وَلَوْ نَشَاءُ لَمَسَخْنَاهُمْ عَلَىٰ مَكَانَتِهِمْ فَمَا اسْتَطَاعُوا مُضِيًّا وَلَا يَرْجِعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-law nashāʾu la-masaḫnāhum ʿalā makānatihim fa-mā staṭāʿū muḍiyyan wa-lā yarjiʿūna
versets.trad: Had We willed, We could have transformed them where they stand, so they could neither go forward nor return.
versets.commentaire: “Had We willed, We could have frozen them in their place.” The soul imprisoned in its disobedience is already, in a sense, immobilized: unable to advance toward God, unable to return to its first innocence. The omnipotence that could petrify bodies reminds the servant of his utter dependence: his very motion, forward or back, is but a loan from Him who holds him.

versets.ar: وَمَن نُّعَمِّرْهُ نُنَكِّسْهُ فِي الْخَلْقِ ۖ أَفَلَا يَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-man nuʿammirhu nunakkishu fī l-ḫalqi a-fa-lā yaʿqilūna
versets.trad: Whomever We grant long life, We reverse him in creation. Do they not then reason?
versets.commentaire: “Whomever We give long life, We turn back in creation: do they not reason?” The decline of old age is a sign inscribed in the body itself: the strength that dwindles teaches that nothing here below is owned outright. To see one's own stature diminish should awaken the heart to seek what does not decline — the face of Him who remains when all else passes.

mots.racine: ن ك س / خ ل ق
mots.def: nakkasa: to reverse, cause to decline.

versets.ar: وَمَا عَلَّمْنَاهُ الشِّعْرَ وَمَا يَنبَغِي لَهُ ۚ إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرٌ وَقُرْآنٌ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: wa-mā ʿallamnāhu sh-shiʿra wa-mā yanbaghī lahu in huwa illā ḏikrun wa-qurʾānun mubīnun
versets.trad: We did not teach him poetry, nor would it befit him. It is only a Reminder and a clear Qurʾān,
versets.commentaire: “We did not teach him poetry: it does not befit him. It is only a Reminder, a clear Qurʾān.” The revealed Word is not the work of human imagination but the pure ḏikr come from on high. Poetry adorns the speech of men; the Qurʾān comes to recall them to themselves and to their Lord — not to charm the ear, but to wake the heart.

mots.racine: ع ل م / ش ع ر
mots.sens: We did not teach him poetry

mots.racine: ق ر أ / ب ي ن

versets.ar: لِّيُنذِرَ مَن كَانَ حَيًّا وَيَحِقَّ الْقَوْلُ عَلَى الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: li-yunḏira man kāna ḥayyan wa-yaḥiqqa l-qawlu ʿalā l-kāfirīna
versets.trad: that it may warn whoever is alive, and that the Word be realized against the disbelievers.
versets.commentaire: “...that it may warn whoever is alive.” The “living” is not merely the one who breathes, but the one whose heart is awake: the Reminder penetrates only the soul still alive to the truth. For the heart dead in denial, the same Word becomes a sentence; thus the Qurʾān separates the living from the dead according to their inner readiness to hear it.

mots.racine: ح ق ق / ق و ل

versets.ar: أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا أَنَّا خَلَقْنَا لَهُم مِّمَّا عَمِلَتْ أَيْدِينَا أَنْعَامًا فَهُمْ لَهَا مَالِكُونَ
versets.tr: a-wa-lam yaraw annā ḫalaqnā lahum mimmā ʿamilat aydīnā anʿāman fa-hum lahā mālikūna
versets.trad: Do they not see that We created for them, among the works of Our hands, cattle which they own?
versets.commentaire: “Do they not see that We created for them livestock which they own?” What man holds to be his possession is in truth a work of the divine hands entrusted to him in trust. To believe oneself owner of what one has received is to forget the Giver; the believer, by contrast, sees in the submitted herd a sign of the providence that provides and a call to gratitude.

mots.racine: ع م ل / ي د ي

versets.ar: وَذَلَّلْنَاهَا لَهُمْ فَمِنْهَا رَكُوبُهُمْ وَمِنْهَا يَأْكُلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ḏallalnāhā lahum fa-minhā rakūbuhum wa-minhā yaʾkulūna
versets.trad: We have subjected them to them; some they ride, and some they eat.
versets.commentaire: “We subjected them to them: some they ride, and some they eat.” The docility of the powerful beast toward fragile man is a sign of the divine order: He who subjected the strong to the weak can subject all things to His will. To recognize this service rendered is to read, in the creature's submission, a reflection of the Creator's sovereignty.

versets.ar: وَلَهُمْ فِيهَا مَنَافِعُ وَمَشَارِبُ ۖ أَفَلَا يَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lahum fīhā manāfiʿu wa-mashāribu a-fa-lā yashkurūna
versets.trad: And they have in them benefits and drinks. Will they not give thanks?
versets.commentaire: “They find in them profits and drinks: will they not give thanks?” Each bounty received is a silent invitation to gratitude. Ingratitude is not only a moral fault: it is blindness to the hand that gives. To give thanks is to let the visible gift lead the heart back to the invisible Giver — and in this the bounty bears its fruit.

versets.ar: وَاتَّخَذُوا مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ آلِهَةً لَّعَلَّهُمْ يُنصَرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ttaḫaḏū min dūni llāhi ālihatan laʿallahum yunṣarūna
versets.trad: Yet they have taken gods besides God, hoping to be helped.
versets.commentaire: “They have taken gods besides God, hoping to be helped by them.” To seek help anywhere but with the One is to turn from the sole source of salvation and cling to the powerless. The idol is whatever the heart turns to, hoping for what God alone can give: the despairing one who trusts secondary causes forgets the One who moves them.

mots.def: dūn: apart from, to the exclusion of.

versets.ar: لَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ نَصْرَهُمْ وَهُمْ لَهُمْ جُندٌ مُّحْضَرُونَ
versets.tr: lā yastaṭīʿūna naṣrahum wa-hum lahum jundun muḥḍarūna
versets.trad: They cannot help them, while they themselves are a host mustered for them.
versets.commentaire: “They cannot help them, while they form for them a mustered host.” A striking reversal: the worshipper believes he serves a god who protects him, and it is he who makes himself the soldier of his impotent idol. Such is the fate of one who places his hope in nothingness: he spends himself for what can do nothing, and on the Day of reckoning neither he nor it will find help.

mots.racine: ط و ع / ن ص ر
ج ن د / ح ض ر

versets.ar: فَلَا يَحْزُنكَ قَوْلُهُمْ ۘ إِنَّا نَعْلَمُ مَا يُسِرُّونَ وَمَا يُعْلِنُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lā yaḥzunka qawluhum innā naʿlamu mā yusirrūna wa-mā yuʿlinūna
versets.trad: So let not their speech grieve you. We know what they conceal and what they reveal.
versets.commentaire: “Let not their speech grieve you: We know what they hide and what they declare.” The servant who leans on God is not touched by the blame of men, for He who knows the secret of hearts is his only judge. The comfort offered to the Prophet holds for every sincere heart: whoever knows that God sees the invisible no longer needs the approval of creatures.

versets.ar: أَوَلَمْ يَرَ الْإِنسَانُ أَنَّا خَلَقْنَاهُ مِن نُّطْفَةٍ فَإِذَا هُوَ خَصِيمٌ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: a-wa-lam yara l-insānu annā ḫalaqnāhu min nuṭfatin fa-iḏā huwa ḫaṣīmun mubīnun
versets.trad: Does man not see that We created him from a drop? Yet behold, he is a manifest adversary!
versets.commentaire: “Does man not see that We created him from a drop? Yet behold, he is a declared disputer!” What a contrast between the humility of the origin — a despised drop — and the arrogance of the creature who dares to contest his Creator. To remember one's infinitesimal beginning heals pride: whoever contemplates whence he came can no longer rise up against Him who drew him from nothingness.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ن ط ف
mots.sens: We created him from a drop

mots.racine: خ ص م / ب ي ن

versets.ar: وَضَرَبَ لَنَا مَثَلًا وَنَسِيَ خَلْقَهُ ۖ قَالَ مَن يُحْيِي الْعِظَامَ وَهِيَ رَمِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-ḍaraba lanā maṯalan wa-nasiya ḫalqahu qāla man yuḥyī l-ʿiẓāma wa-hiya ramīmun
versets.trad: He sets forth a parable for Us and forgets his own creation. He says: “Who will give life to the bones when they are decayed?”
versets.commentaire: “He sets forth a parable for Us and forgets his own creation: Who will revive the bones reduced to dust?” The objection arises from forgetfulness: whoever no longer remembers being drawn from nothingness deems it impossible to be drawn forth again. To measure God's power by one's own impotence is the root of every denial of resurrection.

mots.racine: ض ر ب / م ث ل
mots.sens: he sets forth a parable for Us
mots.def: ḍaraba maṯalan: to give an example.

mots.racine: ن س ي / خ ل ق
ح ي ي / ع ظ م
mots.sens: will give life to the bones

versets.ar: قُلْ يُحْيِيهَا الَّذِي أَنشَأَهَا أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ ۖ وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ خَلْقٍ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: qul yuḥyīhā llaḏī anshaʾahā awwala marratin wa-huwa bi-kulli ḫalqin ʿalīmun
versets.trad: Say: “He who brought them forth the first time will give them life. He knows every creation.”
versets.commentaire: “Say: He who produced them the first time will revive them, and of every creation He is knowing.” The proof lies in the origin itself: He who could bring being forth from nothingness can, all the more, remake it from its remains. The first act of creation is the pledge of the second; and He who knows every scattered atom knows how to gather it.

mots.sens: He produced them the first time

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ع ل م

versets.ar: الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُم مِّنَ الشَّجَرِ الْأَخْضَرِ نَارًا فَإِذَا أَنتُم مِّنْهُ تُوقِدُونَ
versets.tr: allaḏī jaʿala lakum mina sh-shajari l-aḫḍari nāran fa-iḏā antum minhu tūqidūna
versets.trad: He who produced for you, from the green tree, fire — and behold, from it you kindle.
versets.commentaire: “He who drew forth for you, from the green tree, fire.” To make flame spring from the moist sap is the dazzling proof that opposites obey His will: He who unites water and fire in a single branch can well unite life with withered bones. The sign is before our eyes: resurrection is no stranger than this spark hidden in the heart of the green wood.

mots.racine: ش ج ر / خ ض ر

versets.ar: أَوَلَيْسَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ بِقَادِرٍ عَلَىٰ أَن يَخْلُقَ مِثْلَهُم ۚ بَلَىٰ وَهُوَ الْخَلَّاقُ الْعَلِيمُ
versets.tr: a-wa-laysa llaḏī ḫalaqa s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa bi-qādirin ʿalā an yaḫluqa miṯlahum balā wa-huwa l-ḫallāqu l-ʿalīmu
versets.trad: Is not He who created the heavens and the earth able to create the like of them? Yes indeed — He is the All-Creating, the All-Knowing.
versets.commentaire: “Is not He who created the heavens and the earth able to create the like of them? Yes indeed! He is the unceasing Creator, the All-Knowing.” The greater attests the lesser: He who spread out the heavens cannot be deemed powerless to recreate man. The name al-Khallāq tells of creation ever renewed; nothing is exhausted or closed for Him whose work never ceases.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / س م و / أ ر ض
mots.sens: created the heavens and the earth

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ع ل م

versets.ar: إِنَّمَا أَمْرُهُ إِذَا أَرَادَ شَيْئًا أَن يَقُولَ لَهُ كُن فَيَكُونُ
versets.tr: innamā amruhu iḏā arāda shayʾan an yaqūla lahu kun fa-yakūnu
versets.trad: His command, when He wills a thing, is only to say to it “Be,” and it is.
versets.commentaire: “His command, when He wills a thing, is only to say to it: Be! and it is.” Between the divine will and the existence of the thing there is no interval, no effort, no instrument: the word “Be” (kun) is the very act by which nothingness becomes being. All creation is held in this breath; and the resurrection, like the first creation, awaits only this Word to come to pass.

mots.racine: ر و د / ش ي أ

versets.ar: فَسُبْحَانَ الَّذِي بِيَدِهِ مَلَكُوتُ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ
versets.tr: fa-subḥāna llaḏī bi-yadihi malakūtu kulli shayʾin wa-ilayhi turjaʿūna
versets.trad: So glory to Him in whose hand is the dominion of all things, and to whom you will be returned!
versets.commentaire: “Glory to Him in whose hand is the dominion (malakūt) of all things, and to whom you will be returned!” The malakūt is the hidden face of things, their inner reality which God holds and moves: nothing exists or stirs but by this invisible Hand. The sūra is sealed where it opened — upon the Covenant and the secret — : all comes from Him, all returns to Him; and to recognize this return is already to begin returning.

mots.racine: ي د ي / م ل ك
mots.def: yad: hand; malakūt: dominion, the world of the unseen.

mots.racine: ك ل ل / ش ي أ

mots.sens: to Him you will be returned
